sentences.text: Hi, Dolores!
Hello, how are you?
I am doing alright!
How are you?
Pretty good, can't complain.
Great to hear it.
Yes, did you have a lovely weekend?
I did.
How about yourself?
My weekend was good.
I'm trying to remember, did I do anything worse?
They just fly by so quickly.
Unknown.
I know.
It's not fair.
It's not fair, and I feel like, as so many of us, my weekends are kind of just spent, like, running around, like, trying to play catch-up on life, and then.
Right, like, oh, here we go again!
You're real!

Okay, well, I know we… it would have been… we probably met in, like, 2023, and you were working with a consulting company, and Residential One was one of y'all's clients, and now you're the Director of Marketing there, right?
That is correct, yes!
Thank you.
It is… it is great to be back.
Good!
Well, I'm really excited, to show you our Google Business Profile Automation tool.
Okay.
Nope.
We do have a few different tools that we can talk about, and I… like, reputation management is something that we offer.
Okay.
Is that on y'all's radar at all?
It is on my radar.
Yes, indeed.
Definitely.
We certainly want to roll out our reputation management piece, definitely 2026.
You know, some of our properties, of course, you know, in affordable, you know, we definitely struggle with that reputation management piece.
I think our average, Google rating is probably about 3 stars.
Okay, okay, which, you know, is probably not that bad, because.
It's not, right?
Considering.
It's not.
You know….
I feel like unprompted, the reality of human beings, is when they leave a review unprompted, it is because they are angry, and oftentimes, their review has nothing to do with anything.
So….
excuse me.
Nothing that the property could have done.
So, anyway….
Well, I'll say, just for our properties, … Maybe not the case.
You know, I would say most of our, reviews definitely reference operational, right?
Of course, you know, the staff, the team, maybe not so friendly, pest control, maintenance, and yeah, so… and it has a really negative impact on marketing.
Right, I know one of our properties that I was recently working with, you know, we were struggling with the occupancy, but they had a Google rating of about 1.

And I said, well, you know, that's probably it, and I kind of just went through all of the reviews and said, yeah, if I was a prospect, and I was even interested in engaging, I would… I probably would not rent from you guys as well.
Yeah, yeah, okay, okay, which, yeah, I mean, I guess kind of in a nutshell.
Yeah.
you know, why Google business profiles are so important overall, and then….
I agree.
reputation management.
So, okay, cool!
Well, absolutely, we'll chat more about that.
I do have a few questions on the front end, just to make sure that we are, of course, a good fit for y'all, and vice versa.
And then….
into this, we can decide if we want to continue the conversation or pick back up down the road.
Does that sound good?
Yeah.
Okay, good.
Alright!
Well then, yes, when it comes to the amount of properties in the portfolio, how many properties does Residential One oversee?
We have about 125 currently.
Okay?
And, will you remind me, are y'all all owned and managed, or is it third-party?
Or a mix?
It's third party.
Third party, okay?
Got it.
And then… Affordable properties we can absolutely work with.
Senior properties, like, just full transparency, a Google Business Profile automation tool, it's oftentimes not the best fit for,
Okay.
The adult child, we've just noticed, you know, it's kind of a little bit different,
Okay.
Two senior properties.
We do absolutely… I mean, we do absolutely work with, some properties that are, senior, so we can chat more about that, but definitely, like, conventional market rate properties, affordable, great.
Just as far as, you know, because we do know that the adult child is oftentimes a part of that senior living.
Definitely.
So, yeah, it's kind of like their… the adult child's journey to finding a property seems to be a little bit different.
So, anyway, just wanted to kind of let you know that up front.
Thank you.
So when it comes to the market rate and affordable properties, about how many of those are the 125?
Do you know off the top of your head?

So, we don't have, many conventional, even some of our conventional assets, they're a mix of conventional and affordable, so I would probably say the majority of our portfolio is affordable with a mix of conventional.
I would say maybe… … a quarter is senior, maybe a little less than a quarter.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright.
And then, when it comes to your Google business profiles, are all of them claimed and not…
Google?
It's so, like… just as, like, a human being, I have a bone to pick with Google, because I don't understand how their company is so dang huge.
Like, I watch those Waymo self-driving cars, I know that's alphabet, and I'm like, okay, I see you guys doing all these things, where is your support?
Yeah, well, exactly.
There's very little support.

But I will say, so one of the issues is that, so when I was with the, consulting company, we didn't really do a great job of making sure that we claimed a lot of those Google Business pages, so one of my first initiatives coming in was to make sure that we claimed and verified those pages, so I'm still in the process of that, and unfortunately, I don't really have a lot of internal support just yet, so I'm really kind of doing that manually on my own.
So, I know that.
lunch.
That makes sense.
Okay, so you're kind of just starting that journey just now.

That makes sense, which, Godspeed, I wish that it will be easy-peasy, and… but… but too, just so you know, I do feel like this is the first year where Google Business Profiles seem to be really on people's radar, so y'all are definitely, like, not behind in that.
I think, like, they were really big for, like, hotels and restaurants for a long time, and multifamily is kind of… this is the year, it seems, to, like, really be catching on.

I will tell you, and I hate to keep going back to my last position, but this is where I was really able to roll out some more innovative initiatives, and Google Business was one of our first initiatives, and one of the first things was, because we had all of our pages verified, but we hadn't optimized it, and once we started regularly optimizing the pages with content, like, we saw it in real time.
Just that gradual lift month over month over month, you know, we're actually, translates to actual leases.
Right.
Yeah, and like, yeah, the local SEO, it really is….
Oh my gosh.
Yes, it is.
I, have an advertising background, so I totally, like, nerd out on those, like, I'm like, look at those KPIs, no!
Oh my gosh!
I loved it!
So I definitely want to adopt those, you know, that same, innovation.
reached into.
residential one, but it's gonna take some time, but I can't do it on my own, so I'm… I really want to bring in a third party to manage that process.

So right now, I've only, you know, really started with just a core group of properties that are in Lisa, but we know properties that need some assistance and stabilization, but ultimately, the goal is to have the entire portfolio.
Yeah, but no, I think that the way that you are approaching that makes complete sense with the lease-ups and whatnot.
So, at this point, do you know about how many profiles you have claimed and owned?
I would probably say about, mmm, maybe… that I have claimed and owned, maybe about 20?
Oh, that's a good bet!
Okay.
I wrote that.
Because you just came over to Residential One, I think I saw on LinkedIn, like, yeah.
Yeah.
So you're like, yeah, you're putting a good dent in it.
Yes.
Okay, perfect.
So, 20 profiles claimed and owned, and then as far as, like, looking into any vendors who optimize your Google business profiles, have you looked into any others, or are we the first?

So, I spoke to… I know Zumper has a package where they offer Google optimization, and Perk, who is one of our digital partners that manages our lead automation nurturing, and also is going to take over our websites, they also have that.
Google Business piece as well.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Yeah, so, I'm honestly… I'm familiar, of course, with Google, or Zumper's Google.
open.
profile optimization, and you said Perk is also doing it as well.
Yeah.
Yay!
Okay.
Which, yeah, I can't speak to PERC too much.
Okay.

Okay, perfect.
And then, as far as, like, any… Like, PPC, or local SEO initiatives, I know those are kind of an SEM and an SEO approach.
Are y'all doing anything there?
Just starting.
with SEO, but PPC, we do have… I would maybe say about 10 properties currently have paid search campaigns going.
Okay.
Yeah, and I would say one is in a lease-up, and we have a really big campaign going, probably about 5,000….
Okay.
Excellent.
Yes, I'm always happy for you marketing folks on those lease-ups, because, yeah, you don't have to… you just get a budget, and you get….
Exactly, yeah.
I mean, you either spend the money on the front end, or you're gonna spend it on the back end.
Right, okay, okay, great.
And then, so as far as the Google thing, I feel like I've gotten a good bit of information there.
When it comes to reputation management, have y'all started looking around with any vendors there, or is there….
Just started, so at McCormick Barron, we use BirdEye, so that's who I'm familiar with.
Okay, and I… I just met with McGorm McBarron, and I….
Don't you?
Yes, they are one of my accounts, and who I met… Oh!
Was it Madison?
was so lovely.
That sounds correct.
Let me confirm… that's probably my girl… I trained her.
Everything that she knows.
She was lovely!
I enjoyed her so much.
That had to have been her.
Yeah, it was Madison.
Okay, come on!
girl.

We do white-label BirdEye, so if that's something you're familiar with, please know we do all of that.
You would have the same point of contact.
I'm on that, and we… which is kind of silly, we can sometimes, oftentimes, eat BirdEye's price.
Okay, well, we like that.
We'll talk a little bit more about that, but it sounds like you're familiar with it for the most part.
And then, of course, like, the property management software integration is huge.
Yes.
So I will….
M.
At this point in time.

So, okay, so we have Boston Post, which doesn't really have OpenAPI, so we are… thinking about transitioning, hopefully towards the end of the year, we're thinking about Fortress and maybe Funnel for that CRM piece.
Okay, okay, got it.
Yeah, Fortress and Funnel, I know for sure, like, when it comes to the Google Business Profile.
Okay.
side of things, we can, like, custom build things, as needed.
So, integration, I'm not worried about that, but thank you for letting me know, because yes, with the bird-eye piece.
I know.
I will need to confirm that that's an easy integration.
Yeah, Boston Post is, well, what we currently use, no.

Okay, okay.
Well, yeah, please know for Google, even with Boston Post now, we should be able to build a customization, or a custom integration there.
Oh.
Yeah, we have… We have some really incredibly smart human beings on the team.
Okay, because everyone I've talked to, oh no, we can't integrate with Boston Post.
It's just, it's just dead on arrival.
Well, and that's… that is what's fun about being, like, a smaller company, like, it's been so fun.

To see this tool really become what it is, especially you know, working on it these past two years, where Google business profiles weren't too big of a thing, and now they're a huge thing in multifamily, and Google's, you know, placing more and more of an emphasis on those profiles, in regards to increasing local SEO, so it's been really cool to see.

And yeah, we have some incredibly smart people who are able to build these integrations, so, I will, of course, confirm after this call, but there's not been a situation where we've not been able to work with.
Okay, great.
So yeah, just so you know, yeah, reputation, I'll confirm for sure, as well as Google, but it shouldn't be an issue.
Okay.
Okay!
Well… Yeah, going right into it, when it comes to Google, Google still dominates search across the web, with about 87% of renters starting their search for a new apartment on Google as well.
So, when they come across your Google business profiles, we of course want to make sure that y'all look great.
They're able to really, you know, get a good idea of what it's like to live at that property, see the amenities that are offered, so on and so forth.
But beyond making sure that y'all look good, that digital curb appeal, then comes that local SEO.

So, Google rewards relevant, frequent, and unique content on your Google business profiles with that increase in local rank on Google Maps, KPIs like property website traffic, impressions, that sort of thing.

And it's really phenomenal to see I mean, you said that you saw it just manually doing it, so everything that we do at the profile, which, when we do a little deeper dive here, I think you'll be able to be like, okay, holy moly, so that, yes, that probably does absolutely increase the KPIs.
The reason why we created this tool is, we kind of hear, you know, more and more marketing teams are being tasked with stronger resources with.
Mmm.
sorry, strong results with fewer resources.
Sometimes things do fall on the on-site teams, and Lord knows they're overburdened, too.
And then ultimately, the renter experience is not standardized.
So… here is our tool.
So, again, we're going to, integrate with Boston Post, Fortress, Funnel, to do 4 things on your profile daily.
So, first and foremost, we're going to use your photos, and then, leverage AI to create.
Property and amenity-rich posts.
Okay.
We're going to pull over your floor plans with, or sorry, the available floor plans with the starting price.
We'll do that once a day as well.
And there are different ways we can do this, so I'll talk more to that in a second.
We can, manage your Q&A feed, and then we also post about concessions and specials when they're being offered.
So I will show you what that looks like.
… on here, that is… that meant too.

It does.

Of course.

Bizzuto's properties, just to kind of give us a DC property.
Okay.
So if we scroll down, here are going to be those posts.
So, again, what we do is we use your photos, and then, we speak to what is being shown in the photo via AI.
So even if it's something like this, our AI tool does a really great job of, like, you know.
speaking to the bold architectural statement of the building.
But of course, what we want to do here is also highlight, you know, a fitness center.
These are good pictures.
Fitness centers, you know, even, like, a community space.

So yes, any amenities that y'all offer, this is where we definitely want to highlight those, but also just, you know, even if it's not an amenity-rich property, this tool does a great job of just, you know, feeding Google those posts that it wants to see.
Now, is this every single day?
What's the cadence?
Yeah, yeah, of course, sorry.
So we're gonna post once a day.
Okay.
So we have found that that's a sweet spot.
Google likes to see that frequent content.
So we post once a day.
Right now, of course, it's more property-focused.
We are going to have, like, some more templated posts coming up.
Okay.
One thing that I do want to make a note of, because I did see… y'all offer some Concessions occasionally, right?
Okay, great.
So, in addition to our daily posts.
Anytime y'all are offering a concession or special, we will create a post for that once a week while the concession is running.
Okay.

Oh, I like that.
Yeah!
Makes sense.
Okay!
So there are the posts.
And then if we scroll down here, here is going to be our products and amenities feed.
So, we will highlight any, community or in-unit amenities.

highlight all four plans, or maybe not have the starting price, so there are different ways that we can configure this.
Okay.

If you click in.

Dude, my friend.

Definitely.
Any questions on that product speed?

You know, and I know, because I'm in the market looking for a new apartment, I haven't done so in about 10 years, so it's really exciting actually taking that journey as a prospective renter, and so.
So, just….
Knowing, you know, the first thing I go to is the floor plans.
I want to know what floor plans are available, and what is the starting price, so….
Right, yeah, absolutely.
And with that being said, kind of just getting, like, an at-a-glance look at the property.
I really….
This is a newer feature that we've rolled out, and I really like this as well, because, yeah, right in line with that, you can see what else the property entails.
Alright, so… we've done the daily posts, we just went over the products feed, and then lastly, we will go over that Q&A feed.

So… What we've done here… is we looked at the most frequently asked questions to multifamily Google business profiles, and, what we've done to get ahead of a prospective renter even needing to ask a question, because we saw, oftentimes.
A question would be asked, and they'd either never be responded to, or responded to way later.
Is… we looked at the most frequently asked questions, and we basically place those on your profile, and then we edit the answer daily.
So, of course, we would… we would align with y'all on the front end to make sure that we are showcasing the questions that you do want to, have answered on your profile.
These are going to be availability, this will also be where we showcase rent specials, office hours, pet policy, amenities.
So, we place those questions on your profile, and then once a day, we edit the answer, even if it doesn't change, we're just signaling to Google you're super active.
Okay.
Does that make sense?
It does.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Alright, and then that concludes my, Google piece of the spiel.
Any questions there?
No.
Okay.
Straightforward, straight to the point?
Okay, great, because I'm a wordy gal, and one thing that I know to do really well is overcomplicate things when I don't need to.
So great to hear that!
And I know that this might be something that, of course, will… I'll need to confirm with, and it sounds like maybe we can't integrate for the time being.

just to kind of go over this really quickly, again, this is going to be BirdEye that we're white-labeling.

all of this… does all of this sound familiar?

I'm sorry, this is really great pricing, actually.

No, I… This is scary.

Okay.

Okay.
And we're good to go.
Okay.
